2605.10370.title: Autonomous FAIR Digital Objects: From Passive Assertions to Active Knowledge
2605.10370.paper_summary: Scientific knowledge on the Web is published as passive assertions and cannot decide when to validate evidence, reconcile contradictions, or update confidence as findings accumulate. Curation depends on centralised middleware and institutional continuity, but when registries close, active stewardship stops even when data remain online. We advance the concept of Autonomous FAIR Digital Objects (aFDOs) from an abstract idea to an operational model, to offer a route from passive scientific publication toward accountable, standards-aligned automation that can outlive its publishing institutions. aFDO augments FDOs with three capabilities anchored in Semantic Web standards, namely 1) a policy layer over RDF-star aligned with PROV-O, SHACL, and ODRL for portable condition-action rules, 2) an announcement layer over ActivityStreams 2.0 that bounds per-announcement evaluation cost, and 3) an agreement layer that resolves multi-source contradictions through reputation and confidence weighted agreement under a bounded adversarial model. We provide a formal definition that distinguishes policy specifications, event handlers, and communication interfaces. We evaluate an open reference implementation on 4,305 FDOs grounded in rare-disease ontologies, namely ClinVar, HPO, and Orphanet, combined with controlled synthetic observations. The consensus mechanism resolves 56.3% of 3,914 naturally occurring ClinVar conflicts where multiple submitters disagree and an expert panel has subsequently adjudicated. Under Sybil, collusion, and poisoning attacks, the mechanism degrades gracefully within its design Byzantine-tolerance bound (f < n/5), and fails as predicted beyond that bound.
2605.10370.paper_summary_zh: 科學知識在網路上以被動的陳述形式發表，無法決定何時驗證證據、調和矛盾或隨著發現的積累更新信心。策展依賴於集中式中介軟體和機構的持續性，但當登記處關閉時，主動管理停止，即使數據仍然在線。我們將自主 FAIR 數位物件 (aFDOs) 的概念從抽象思想推進到運作模型，以提供一條從被動科學出版走向負責任、符合標準的自動化的路徑，這種自動化能夠超越其出版機構。aFDO 在 FDO 的基礎上增強了三項能力，這些能力基於語義網標準，分別是 1) 一個政策層，基於與 PROV-O、SHACL 和 ODRL 對齊的 RDF-star，提供可攜帶的條件-行動規則，2) 一個公告層，基於 ActivityStreams 2.0，限制每次公告的評估成本，以及 3) 一個協議層，通過聲譽和信心加權的協議，在有限的對抗模型下解決多來源的矛盾。我們提供了一個正式定義，以區分政策規範、事件處理程序和通信介面。我們在 4,305 個基於罕見疾病本體的 FDO 上評估了一個開放的參考實現，這些本體包括 ClinVar、HPO 和 Orphanet，並結合了受控的合成觀察。共識機制解決了 3,914 個自然發生的 ClinVar 矛盾中 56.3% 的情況，這些情況中多個提交者之間存在分歧，並且專家小組隨後已進行裁決。在 Sybil、共謀和毒化攻擊下，該機制在其設計的拜占庭容忍邊界內 (f < n/5) 平穩降級，並在超出該邊界時按預測失效。
2605.10370.author: Zeyd Boukhers et.al.
2605.10370.authors: Zeyd Boukhers, Oya Beyan, Cong Yang, Christoph Lange
